status: Fluree v4.0.5 vs QLever. This is the DBLP KG+citations dataset (~1.57B triples), NOT the paper's ~502M core, so it is NOT directly comparable to the published SPARQLoscope table (engine-vs-engine on one box).

dataset.name: DBLP-KG (bibliography + OpenCitations citations)
dataset.description: dblp_KG_with_associated_data.tar — DBLP bibliography plus OpenCitations citation data

methodology.notes: QLever result cache disabled + cleared before each query so it re-executes (matches Fluree, which has no result cache). Reference engine for the correctness check: QLever.

fluree.label: Fluree
fluree.config: server cache auto (~63 GB); inline-indexed ledger

import.notes: parallelized import (16 parse threads + parallel secondary index builds)
import.phases: parse+commit 633 s, index build 336 s (3 secondaries built in parallel alongside SPOT)
sequential parse ~50 min (parallel parse rejected on this layout) + permutation build

caveats: Triple-count delta: Fluree 1,574,283,728 vs QLever 1,574,230,030 (+0.0034%) — full-scan counts differ slightly; folded into 'within data delta' agreement.
NOT comparable to the published SPARQLoscope table (that used the ~502M DBLP core, no citations). Absolute times are this-box-only.
Fluree's import (1,047 s, parallelized) vs QLever (3,937 s) — QLever handicapped by the forced sequential parse on this concatenated shard layout.

Documented engine-semantics differences (⚠, both defensible, Fluree spec-correct): filter-many-results — FILTER(?s != ?o) over blank-node subjects; both agree the data is identical (0 self-typed), Fluree keeps the 113.9M bnode rows (spec-correct per RDFterm-equal), QLever drops them. date-day / date-month — DAY()/MONTH() on a gYear literal (edge case). group-by-implicit-string-max / -min — string collation order.

Five queries differ from QLever by ±1 row (regex-prefix-1/2/3, transitive-path-large/small-join-and-plus) — boundary/edge differences in regex-prefix and transitive-path handling; the other 100 queries agree.
